steps.title: Identify the structure on each side
steps.content: The sentence opens with "Defined by a disorganized molecular structure," — an introductory participial phrase. The comma signals that the very next noun must be the thing that is defined by this disorganized molecular structure.

steps.title: Apply the rule: a participial modifier must touch the noun it modifies
steps.content:
The opening phrase describes something with a disorganized molecular structure. That must be amorphous ice, not ice Ih (which has an orderly crystalline structure).
  • "ice Ih differs from amorphous ice in that it possesses the thermal energy to form crystals.": "Defined by a disorganized molecular structure, ice Ih differs..." — ice Ih has an organized hexagonal structure, so this is a dangling modifier. ✗
  • "ice Ih contains crystals, whereas amorphous ice, which lacks the thermal energy to form them, does not.": "Defined by a disorganized molecular structure, ice Ih contains crystals..." — again ice Ih follows the modifier, contradicting the passage. ✗
  • "the lack of thermal energy in amorphous ice explains its inability to form the crystals found in ice Ih.": "Defined by a disorganized molecular structure, the lack of thermal energy..." — an abstract concept cannot be "defined by a molecular structure"; dangling modifier. ✗
  • "amorphous ice lacks the thermal energy to form the crystals found in ice Ih.": "Defined by a disorganized molecular structure, amorphous ice lacks the thermal energy to form the crystals found in ice Ih." — amorphous ice is the subject and correctly receives the modifier. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
